title: Trump administration says it’s deferring $1B in Medicaid payments to California and Minnesota
summary: The Trump administration said it was deferring more than $1 billion in Medicaid payments to Minnesota and California because of “suspected fraud and noncompliance.”

title: STAT+: Rare disease drugmakers try to avoid Trump price cuts
summary: Plus, GOP voters want a crackdown on health care fraud, and how PBMs are skirting state laws.

title: Agios scraps sickle cell drug
summary: The study failure costs Agios a chance to one-up Novo Nordisk and represents yet another setback in a disease that’s frustrated drugmakers of late.

title: Novo sues Lilly, alleging ‘misleading’ advertising of obesity drugs
summary: The lawsuit follows a cease-and-desist request and argues Lilly is using “deceptive” ads to claim its GLP-1 products are broadly superior to Novo’s medicines. Lilly countered that its campaign is…

title: Opinion: Pete Hegseth, ‘The Odyssey,’ and the testosterone myth
summary: The poet didn’t say “sing to me, Muse, of a man with optimized endocrine panels," writes urologist Elizabeth Dray.

title: Novartis climbs on upbeat drug sales as key readouts loom
summary: A return to quarterly sales growth boosted shares higher on Tuesday, though a handful of coming study results will have a bigger impact on the company’s long-term outlook.

title: STAT+: Advocates say federal funding is being cut from groups fighting HIV
summary: The administration is shifting some funding to states, but community groups are set to lose out.

title: Is there an exodus underway at Epic?
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: executive departures at Epic Systems, new CEO takes over at Nabla, and more health tech news.

title: STAT+: Broad Institute, partners unveil new initiative to develop gene therapies for rare diseases
summary: The Broad Institute, Boston Children’s Hospital, and Maine’s Jackson Laboratory announced a collaboration to develop rare disease gene therapies.

summary: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly over GLP-1s, Medicare pricing plans, and more biotech news from The Readout

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Novo suing Lilly over ‘deceptive’ ads, a fugitive’s secret life in biotech, and more
summary: Novo Nordisk sued Eli Lilly for allegedly running “deceptive” ads to make its obesity and diabetes drugs appear more effective

title: STAT+: Nabla’s new CEO on plan to gain market share in a competitive AI scribe environment
summary: Ambient scribe developer Nabla said Brian Manning will be the Paris-based company's new CEO as it looks to grow in a highly competitive market.

title: STAT+: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly over GLP-1 drug ads it calls ‘deceptive’
summary: Novo Nordisk sued Eli Lilly for allegedly running a “deceptive” ad campaign that uses “outdated clinical trials” to make its obesity and diabetes drugs appear more effective.

summary: The latest on cyclospora, THC sodas at the grocery store, and more health news from Morning Rounds

title: STAT+: Makers of rare disease drugs seek exemption from Trump’s price-cut pilots
summary: A coalition of biotech firms is lobbying to exclude orphan drugs from new federal pilot programs aimed at lowering Medicare drug costs.

title: Opinion: Science and the public are learning the wrong lessons from Covid
summary: “Post-pandemic polarization threatens to lock our memories into attractive, untrue conclusions that will leave us unprotected next time,” write Marc Lipsitch and Sara Cody.

title: Opinion: How a controversial rule could hurt the NIH’s ability to manage risk
summary: The proposed rule could “weaken the NIH’s benign influence over research risks,” writes Doni Bloomfield.

title: THC drinks are sold beside sodas, iced tea, and even chocolate milk, often in violation of state laws
summary: THC drinks, which have exploded in popularity in recent years, are sold in retail stores beside sodas, iced tea, and even chocolate milk, often in violation of state laws.

title: FDA still focused on lettuce supplier as source of parasite, despite faulty test result
summary: The FDA said Monday it is still focused on lettuce from Taylor Farms as the source of a multistate outbreak of a diarrhea-causing parasite, despite a faulty test result.

title: Tempus to acquire cancer test maker Personalis for $1.5B
summary: Tempus AI’s planned acquisition follows recent interest in blood-based cancer tests across the medtech industry.

title: Pain drugmaker Latigo outlines plans to go public
summary: The Westlake-founded company specializes in non-opioid drugs that target sodium ion channels, and is now adding to what’s already been a busy month for the biotech IPO queue.

summary: Medicare proposes to reduce payments to hospitals for certain drugs and imaging services.

summary: A former doctor accused of sexual assault but on the run for 20 years appears to have been living a secret life as a biotech executive.

summary: This podcast explores how clinical trial supplies move from early planning to global delivery, ensuring investigational medicines reach patients safely and on time.

title: Compounders maintain a firm grip on the GLP-1 market
summary: Despite an FDA crackdown, numerous pharma lawsuits and possible safety risks, nearly 1 in 5 patients are still taking cheaper weight-loss alternatives.
